message:
Implement DNS resolver with /etc/hosts and /etc/services file-based backends

Major expansion of the resolv module (~530 new lines) implementing
file-based hostname resolution backends:

New functionality:
- parse_hosts_line(): parses /etc/hosts format with comment stripping,
  whitespace handling, and IPv4/IPv6 address validation
- lookup_hosts(): hostname lookup against /etc/hosts content with
  family filtering (AF_INET, AF_INET6, AF_UNSPEC)
- parse_services_line(): parses /etc/services format (name port/proto)
- lookup_service(): service name to port resolution with protocol matching
- getaddrinfo_impl(): full POSIX-compatible getaddrinfo implementation
  supporting numeric addresses, /etc/hosts, /etc/services, AI_PASSIVE,
  AI_NUMERICHOST, AI_NUMERICSERV flags, and proper EAI error codes

Also added EAI error code constants (EAI_NONAME, EAI_SERVICE, EAI_FAMILY)
and AF family constants matching POSIX values.

Updated README.md with declared replacement level documentation
(L0 — Interpose) and release tag format convention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -52,6 +52,12 @@ Support-matrix applicability rule:
 - `Implemented` + `RawSyscall` symbols apply to both artifacts.
 - `GlibcCallThrough` + `Stub` symbols apply to `Interpose` only.
 
+## Declared Replacement Level (Machine-Checked)
+
+Source of truth: `tests/conformance/replacement_levels.json`.
+Declared replacement level claim: **L0 — Interpose**.
+Release tag format: `v<semver>-L<level>` (for example `v0.1.0-L0` at current level).
+
